vehicles.slot: Column shifter (2000-2002): slot on steering column. Console shifter (2003+): slot at side of console shifter base
shift: COLUMN SHIFT (2000-2002): Ignition ON. Find slot on column near shifter. Insert key, press down. Press brake. Move column shifter to N.
CONSOLE SHIFT (2003+): Find slot at side of console shifter. Remove cap. Insert key, press down. Press brake. Move to N.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: 4WD: transfer case to 2H before towing on road.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: transfer case MUST be in 2H before towing.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from front (rear/drive wheels roll on ground). Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (Autotrac) — 2000-2002 may have manual lever
positions: 2WD (2H) — rear wheels only, for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD engagement (NOT safe for towing)
4WD Hi (4H) — part-time 4WD high range (NOT safe for dry-road towing)
4WD Lo (4L) — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH or come to a complete stop.
ELECTRIC (push-button): Press the 2WD button on the dash or console. Wait for the 2WD indicator lamp to illuminate.
MANUAL LEVER (older models): Stop completely. Move the transfer case floor lever firmly to the 2H position.
Confirm the 2WD indicator lamp is ON before lifting rear wheels.
Verify all 4WD indicator lights are OFF.
NEVER tow in 4H, 4WD Auto, or 4L — serious driveline damage will result.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Transfer Case Must Be in 2WD — Not Just Neutral
knownIssues.detail: Operators sometimes shift the transfer case to Neutral thinking that's safe to tow. The driveshaft is still connected in TC Neutral on these models. Transfer case MUST be in 2WD (2H) for wheel-lift towing, not just neutral.

knownIssues.title: Heavy Vehicle — Equipment Ratings
knownIssues.detail: Escalade curb weight ranges 5,600–6,200 lbs depending on year and trim. Verify your wheel-lift and strapping equipment is rated appropriately before attempting to lift.

battery.warnings: Heavy vehicle. Verify TC is in 2WD before lifting.

airSuspension.description: Magnetic Ride Control / Automatic Level Control air suspension.
towingNotes: Battery jump to normalize if vehicle is low.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector on console — slot near base or under a trim panel
shift: Locate trim panel near electronic selector. Remove or pry.
Insert key into slot, press down.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: Escalade IQ EV (2025+): 12V only — flatbed required.
vehicles.warn: Escalade IQ EV: FLATBED ONLY. 4WD: transfer case to 2H.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from front (rear/drive wheels roll on ground). Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only, for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD (NOT safe for towing)
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD (NOT safe for dry-road towing)
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Turn the rotary transfer case dial (or press 2WD button) on the center console to 2WD.
Wait for the 2WD indicator to illuminate on the instrument cluster.
Verify all 4WD lights are OFF before lifting rear wheels.
Escalade IQ EV (2025+): FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain, no transfer case.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Electronic Selector Requires Power
knownIssues.detail: 4th and 5th gen Escalade uses a fully electronic gear selector. With a dead battery, the shift lock slot must be used. The slot is concealed under a trim panel — locate it before the job if possible.

knownIssues.title: Escalade IQ EV (2025+) — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: The 2025 Escalade IQ is a full EV. FLATBED ONLY. No transfer case. 12V auxiliary battery only for electronics. Never touch the HV propulsion battery.

battery.warnings: Escalade IQ EV: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release.

airSuspension.description: Magnetic Ride Control / Automatic Level Control air suspension.
towingNotes: Battery jump to normalize if vehicle is low.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock (push-button start models). Will engage without ignition — use flatbed or ensure steering is not locked during tow.

neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock (push-button start models). Will engage without ignition — use flatbed or ensure steering is not locked during tow.

neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock (push-button start models). Will engage without ignition — use flatbed or ensure steering is not locked during tow.

neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release.

airSuspension.description: Magnetic Ride Control / Automatic Level Control air suspension.
towingNotes: Battery jump to normalize if vehicle is low.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock (push-button start models). Will engage without ignition — use flatbed or ensure steering is not locked during tow.

neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from FRONT. Rear (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If front-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the FRONT wheels and tow rear-first.
AWD VARIANT: Use flatbed. If no flatbed, wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock (push-button start models). Will engage without ignition — use flatbed or ensure steering is not locked during tow.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: WITH POWER: Press brake. Select N.
WITHOUT POWER: Find slot near selector base. Insert key, press down. Press brake. Select N.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. 12V auxiliary only.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock (push-button start models). Will engage without ignition — use flatbed or ensure steering is not locked during tow.
